projects.tagline: Learn to read population pyramids and predict a country's future using real demographic data
projects.full_description: In this project, you will analyze a population data table to produce a population pyramid and explore the demographic story it tells. Population pyramids reveal a country's past through the effects of war, policy, and migration, while forecasting its future growth, stability, or decline.You will examine demographic data, create hand-drawn sketches, and use AI feedback to refine your work. Then you will collaborate with AI to create an infographic that communicates the population's story visually. Your analysis will include calculating dependency ratios, identifying the Demographic Transition Model stage, recommending a business investment, and predicting future challenges based on the data.By the end of this project, you will enhance your Applied AI skills, and understand that a population pyramid is not just a chart but a narrative of human experience with predictive power for those who learn to read it.

projects.tagline: Build a personal net worth calculator that banks actually use
projects.full_description: What are you actually worth? Net worth is one of the most important numbers in personal finance because it tells you the full picture of your financial health by combining everything you own with everything you owe. In this project, you will build a personal net worth calculator in Google Sheets that transforms raw financial data into meaningful insights about wealth and financial stability.You will start by entering career and income information, then catalog your assets (what you own) and liabilities (what you owe). From there, you will write formulas that calculate key financial metrics including net worth, ownership ratios, and debt-to-income ratios. These calculations are the same ones used by banks when deciding whether to approve loans and by financial advisors when helping clients plan for the future.By the end of this project, you will have a functional financial tool and a deeper understanding of how wealth is measured. More importantly, you will practice the kind of analytical thinking that helps people make informed decisions about money throughout their lives.

Use AI to learn how to write the formulas in cell range F3:F5

projects.tagline: Master the note-taking system used by top executives and students worldwide
projects.full_description: Cornell Notes is a proven note-taking system that helps you organize information, identify key concepts, and reflect on your learning. This method divides your notes into three sections: Cues/Questions for key terms and study prompts, Notes for main ideas and details, and Summary for synthesis and reflection. You'll apply this system to the video "Essentialism: Animated Book Summary" by Greg McKeown, creating notes that demonstrate your comprehension of the seven principles of essentialism and how they apply to achieving goals more effectively. You can also use this NoteBookLM to gain understanding.Learning Objective:Web/Global IT: How can focusing on fewer, high-impact features lead to more successful web projects than trying to include everything?Finance: How can prioritizing essential financial goals and saying no to non-essential spending lead to greater financial success?
what_students_learn: This information is required for your teacher, and for GradeFlow to evaluate your assignment within the context of the class, topic, and learning objective
Row 1: Begin with the first main idea or concept from your source
Row 2: Begin with the first main idea or concept from your source
Row 3: Begin with the first main idea or concept from your source
Your notes will be evaluated for completeness and alignment with the learning objective

projects.tagline: Create a resume that stands out to college admissions and employers
projects.full_description: You will create a polished resume suitable for a high school that showcases your academic achievements, experiences, and skills. This resume will help you organize your qualifications into a coherent story for potential employers or internship coordinators. By the end of this project, you'll have a polished document that represents who you are and where you're headed professionally.

tasks.description: Add you phone number, email address, and location. Separate each element with a delimiter like a pipe, slash, etc....Phone number must use the Common Display Format for North American, 3-3-4 split for...

sections.name: Summary Section
sections.description: Write a clear and concise summary statement that tells employers who you are, including your grade level, a standout trait, and your intended path or interest. Name one or two strengths or skills that match the role you want. Add one proof point, such as your GPA, a certification, a project, an award, or a measurable result. Keep it specific, include numbers when you can, tailor it to the role, and avoid buzzwords, fluff, and long lists. Aim for 1 to 2 sentences, roughly 20 - 30 words.EXAMPLES:Dependable junior seeking part-time retail role; 3.6 GPA; school store cashier handling $500 daily. Available evenings and weekends.STEM-focused senior seeking data internship; Python and Excel; built a survey dashboard used by 60 students that improved planning decisions.Motivated senior seeking peer tutor role; AP Calculus score 5; raised tutees’ quiz averages by 12 percent through targeted review.

projects.tagline: Master the 50-30-20 budgeting rule with a hands-on Google Sheets project
projects.full_description: Are your students struggling to visualize real-world money management or dreading their next spreadsheet lesson? Turn financial literacy into a hands-on adventure with this interactive 50-30-20 Budget project built entirely in Google Sheets. It’s the perfect way to bridge the gap between essential life skills and digital competency in a format digital natives understand. This comprehensive resource guides students through 27 engaging activities designed to build a functioning monthly budget from scratch. Inside, you will find: Step-by-Step Project Setup: Students start by choosing a career, researching realistic salaries, and calculating tax/net income. Core Spreadsheet Skills: Practical tasks that teach writing formulas, using Sum functions, formatting data, and mastering absolute cell references. Budgeting Categories: Dedicated sections for Needs (Housing, Groceries), Wants (Entertainment), and Financials (Emergency Funds), applying the 50/30/20 rule. Visuals & Analysis: Students generate automatic Pie Charts to visualize spending and complete reflection activities to analyze their budget's realism. Stop spending your evenings creating complex practice sheets from scratch. This fully digital resource is designed for seamless implementation, engaging students with content that matters to their future while reinforcing technical standards. You get a high-impact, dual-purpose lesson that saves you time and empowers your classroom. Ready to teach financial independence? Purchase this ready-to-use Google Sheets resource today and transform how your students think about money and data!

metadata.pitch: Master the 50-30-20 budgeting rule with a hands-on Google Sheets project
metadata.outcome: Complete budget spreadsheet with 27 activities covering needs, wants, and savings

projects.tagline: Use latitude and longitude like a GPS to find countries around the world
projects.full_description: In this project, you will use latitude and longitude with Google Maps to identify a country. Then you'll review the questions in the Google Doc template before updating it. You'll learn how latitude and longitude coordinates pinpoint a country’s absolute location and how its neighbors and landmarks illustrate relative location. For each of ten coordinate pairs, you will:Find the country where the lines intersect and enter its name.List the countries or bodies of water in a specific direction (north, south, east, west, etc.).Identify and describe a famous physical or cultural place in the region within the target country, using at least two specific, verifiable details.Provide map evidence by pasting a Google Maps URL or inserting a map screenshot, as directed in each table.All of your answers should reflect the Five Themes of Geography. Do not alter any of the coordinate or question rows in the template.
what_students_learn: Table#01: Ensure that the place you describe is located in the country listed in Row 2 and directly answers the question in Row 5
Table#02: Ensure that the place you describe is located in the country listed in Row 2 and directly answers the question in Row 5
Table#03: Ensure that the place you describe is located in the country listed in Row 2 and directly answers the question in Row 5
Table#04: Ensure that the place you describe is located in the country listed in Row 2 and directly answers the question in Row 5
Table#05: Ensure that the place you describe is located in the country listed in Row 2 and directly answers the question in Row 5

sections.name: Absolute & Relative Locations
sections.description: You’ll start by plotting each site’s absolute location using its latitude and longitude coordinates. Next, you’ll describe its relative location by naming which countries or bodies of water lie to the north, south, east, west, and in the diagonal (NE, NW, SE, SW) directions. Then you’ll pick one physical feature (for example, a mountain range or a wetland ecosystem) or one cultural landmark (like a historic monument or major city) in that area, and support each choice with at least two clear, verifiable facts. Finally, you’ll embed a Google Maps URL or screenshot to prove your location work and sharpen your GIS and spatial-reasoning skills.For each task, you must do the following.Enter the correct country name.List neighboring countries or bodies of water in the direction specified.Describe a famous place in the region of the country, with two clear facts.Add the required map link or screenshot for that country.

sections.name: Physical & Cultural Place: Teacher Graded
sections.description: Your teacher will evaluate the Physical/Human place descriptions you wrote in Row 6 and the image/URL you input into Row 8 of each table.Matches the country specified in Row 2.Directly answers the question in Row 5.Includes at least two specific, verifiable facts about the place.Description is well written, with no spelling or grammar errors.Image or URL matches the country in Row 2.These are Teacher-Graded tasks, so you will not receive scoring and feedback until after you submit your work to your teacher.
